attractions.name: Salt Lake Tabernacle (also known as the Mormon Tabernacle)
attractions.description: The Salt Lake Tabernacle, completed in 1867, is an iconic domed auditorium on Temple Square known for its world-class acoustics and historic 11,623-pipe organ. It serves as the long-time home of the world-famous Tabernacle Choir at Temple Square and is an architectural masterpiece featuring a massive self-supporting roof built with pioneer-era ingenuity. National Park Service, Ensign Peak Foundation
attractions.address: 50 N West Temple St, Salt Lake City, UT 84150-9001
attractions.region: Temple Square, Downtown Salt Lake City
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:00 a.m. – 9:00 p.m.; Organ Recitals: Mon–Sat at 12:00 p.m., Sun at 2:00 p.m.; Choir Rehearsals: Thu at 7:30 p.m. – 9:30 p.m.; Sunday Broadcast: 9:30 a.m. (doors open at 8:15 a.m.) Church of Jesus Christ, The Tabernacle Choir
attractions.days_open: Monday–Sunday
attractions.admission: Free (All events including choir rehearsals and organ recitals are free of charge) The Tabernacle Choir, Church of Jesus Christ
highlights: Home of the world-renowned Tabernacle Choir at Temple Square
Features a massive pipe organ with 11,623 pipes, one of the largest in the world
Renowned "pin-drop" acoustics that allow a sound as quiet as a dropped pin to be heard clearly from the back of the hall
Unique oval dome-shaped roof built without interior support columns using a bridge-building truss design
Host to the weekly "Music & the Spoken Word" broadcast, the world\
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30-45 minutes early for the Sunday morning Tabernacle Choir broadcast to ensure seating. Guests must be in their seats by 9:15 a.m. for the live 9:30 a.m. performance. Thursday evening rehearsals offer a more casual way to hear the choir and allow visitors to come and go. The Tabernacle Choir, Viator

attractions.name: Salt Lake Temple Church of Jesus Christ
attractions.description: The Salt Lake Temple is the iconic centerpiece of Temple Square and the largest temple of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. Completed in 1893 after forty years of construction, its distinctive six-spire Gothic-style architecture serves as a global symbol of the faith. It is currently undergoing a comprehensive seismic and interior renovation to preserve the historic structure for future generations Wikipedia, Temple Square Official.

attractions.city: Salt Lake City Church of Jesus Christ

attractions.admission: Free (some tours and exhibits require free ticket reservations) Temple Square FAQ
highlights: Distinctive six-spire Gothic-style architecture built from quartz monzonite Wikipedia
Largest Latter-day Saint temple by floor area, spanning over 253,000 square feet Church of Jesus Christ Temples
Historical centerpiece of the 10-acre Temple Square in downtown Salt Lake City Wikipedia
Historic 40-year construction period (1853–1893) by early pioneers Visit Utah

attractions.tips: While the temple interior is typically closed to the general public, the new Temple Square Visitors' Center (opened May 2026) offers a guided 'Inside a Temple' tour with full-scale replicas of ceremonial rooms. It is recommended to download the Temple Square app to manage free reservations for tours and access up-to-date maps of areas affected by the ongoing renovation Visit Utah, Temple Square Official.

attractions.name: Delta Center
attractions.description: Delta Center, home to the NBA's Utah Jazz and NHL's Utah Mammoth, is the premier sports and entertainment venue in downtown Salt Lake City. The arena hosts over 320 events annually, including professional sports, concerts, and family shows, welcoming approximately 1.8 million guests each year (Delta Center).
attractions.address: 301 West South Temple, Salt Lake City, UT 84101
attractions.hours: Box Office (Event Days): 1:00 PM – varies by event; Team Store: Mon-Sat 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM (11:00 AM – 3:00 PM on game days); Arena Doors: Typically open 90 minutes before events (Delta Center, Jazz Team Store)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Team Store open Mon-Sat; Arena open for scheduled events)
attractions.admission: Utah Jazz ($50-$150), Utah Mammoth ($100-$200), Suites ($4,000-$12,000), Children under 24 months: Free (Ticketmaster, Delta Center)
highlights: Home to the NBA's Utah Jazz and NHL's Utah Mammoth (Wikipedia)
Features a signature 14-foot tall, illuminated black Jazz Note statue on the main plaza (Delta Center)
Undergoing a multi-year transformational renovation starting in 2025 (Delta Center)
Equipped with energy-generating solar panels on the roof (Delta Center)
State-of-the-art sensory room and dedicated mother's lounges (Delta Center)
Seating capacity of over 18,000 for basketball and 17,000 for hockey (Delta Center)
attractions.tips: The arena is completely cash-free; only digital and card payments are accepted. There is a strict no-bag policy except for clutches (4"x6"x2") and medical/diaper bags (14"x14"x6"), which must enter via Door 5. For easy access, use the TRAX light rail Green or Blue lines to the Arena Station (Delta Center, Radical Storage).

attractions.name: Zions Bank Broadway Office
attractions.description: The Zions Bank Broadway Office is a prominent financial center located in the heart of downtown Salt Lake City. Occupying the historic 15-story building formerly known as the J.C. Penney Building, it serves as a key hub for personal, business, and wealth management services. The branch is a staple of the city's commercial district and is deeply integrated into the local business community.

attractions.description: Clark Planetarium is a premier destination for space and science education, featuring over 10,000 square feet of interactive exhibits and state-of-the-art theaters. It serves as a community hub for STEM learning, offering immersive dome and IMAX experiences that inspire curiosity about the universe. Official Site, Wikipedia

highlights: Newton’s Daydream: A two-story audio-kinetic ball sculpture in the lobby. Expedia
Science on a Sphere: Interactive projections of planetary data on a 6-foot globe. Expedia

attractions.name: The Utah Military Museum at Historic Fort Douglas
attractions.description: The Utah Military Museum at Historic Fort Douglas honors and preserves the rich military history of Utah and the fort itself through engaging exhibits and educational programs. Visitors can explore a vast collection of artifacts, weapons, and vehicles spanning from the Civil War era to modern-day military engagements.
attractions.address: 32 Potter Street, Salt Lake City, Utah 84113
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Open by appointment only; Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Thursday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Sunday: Closed
attractions.days_open: Wednesday - Saturday (Public); Tuesday (By appointment)
attractions.admission: Free (donations encouraged)
highlights: Outdoor static display featuring tanks, helicopters, and artillery pieces
Comprehensive exhibits on the history of Fort Douglas since its founding in 1862
Specialized displays on the 10th Mountain Division and Utah's role in World War II
Historic sandstone and red brick barracks buildings dating back to 1875
Personalized tours often led by knowledgeable veteran volunteers and docents
attractions.tips: Allow time to explore the outdoor vehicle displays; wear comfortable walking shoes. Admission is free, but donations are greatly appreciated to help maintain the historic collections. The museum is located on a historic but functional military post, so be sure to respect all posted boundaries.

attractions.name: Bonneville Shoreline Trail
attractions.description: The Bonneville Shoreline Trail (BST) is a mixed-use recreation trail that roughly follows the ancient shoreline of prehistoric Lake Bonneville. It offers a unique urban-wildlife interface, providing recreational activities such as hiking, mountain biking, and nature study with unparalleled views of the Salt Lake Valley. (SLC.gov, Wikipedia)

High accessibility with 22 trailhead access points within the Salt Lake City portion alone (SLC.gov)
Diverse wildlife watching opportunities and native high-desert vegetation (SLC.gov)
attractions.tips: Bring plenty of water as there is minimal shade on the foothills, and always yield to uphill travelers and practice trail etiquette. (Utah.com, Airial Travel)

attractions.name: City Creek Canyon Park and Nature Preserve
attractions.description: City Creek Canyon is a vital nature preserve and protected watershed located just minutes from downtown Salt Lake City. It offers a 5.9-mile paved road and parallel dirt trails that provide a serene escape for hikers, runners, and cyclists. The canyon is known for its lush vegetation, bubbling stream, and diverse wildlife, serving as a primary source of high-quality drinking water for the city.

attractions.hours: Monday: 8:30 AM – 10:00 PM; Tuesday: 8:30 AM – 10:00 PM; Wednesday: 8:30 AM – 10:00 PM; Thursday: 8:30 AM – 10:00 PM; Friday: 8:30 AM – 10:00 PM; Saturday: 8:30 AM – 10:00 PM; Sunday: 8:30 AM – 10:00 PM (Closes at 8:00 PM on holidays)

attractions.admission: Free for pedestrians and bicyclists; $3.00 for private vehicles (Note: Private vehicle access currently suspended during Water Treatment Plant construction)
highlights: A 5.9-mile scenic paved roadway popular for car-free recreation on designated days.
The 'Hobbit Fountain,' a natural warm spring that keeps surrounding foliage green year-round.
Rotary Park, featuring historic picnic areas and the secluded sitting spot known as 'The Grotto.'
Exceptional wildlife viewing opportunities, including frequent sightings of deer, moose, and elk.
A network of singletrack trails and access to the regional Bonneville Shoreline Trail.
Direct pedestrian access from the city center through the historic Memory Grove Park.
attractions.tips: Observe the summer schedule where bicycles are allowed on odd-numbered days and vehicles on even-numbered days (when permitted). Dogs must remain on-leash and are strictly prohibited above the water treatment plant (Site #16) to protect the watershed. Be aware of ongoing construction at the water treatment plant, which may impact road access and picnic site availability through 2027.

attractions.name: Ensign Peak Nature Park
attractions.description: Ensign Peak Nature Park is a historic landmark and natural area located in the foothills north of downtown Salt Lake City. It is famous for its short but steep hiking trail that leads to a summit monument, offering panoramic views of the Salt Lake Valley and Great Salt Lake. The site holds significant historical and religious importance as the location where Brigham Young and other pioneer leaders surveyed the valley to plan the layout of the city in 1847.
attractions.address: 1002 N Ensign Vista Dr, Salt Lake City, UT 84103
attractions.hours: 5:00 AM – 10:00 AM (Daily)
attractions.days_open: Monday-Sunday (Daily)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Short 1-mile round-trip hike to the summit monument
Breathtaking panoramic views of Salt Lake City and the Great Salt Lake
Historic stone monument at the peak marking the 1847 survey site
Interpretive signs detailing the history and natural environment of the area
Entrance plaza with a world map and pedestals representing early pioneers
Direct connection to the Bonneville Shoreline Trail system
attractions.tips: The hike is short but steep, so wear comfortable shoes and bring water. It is a popular spot for sunset and sunrise views, but keep in mind that the park officially closes at 10:00 PM. Parking is limited to street parking on Ensign Vista Drive or nearby residential streets. Dogs are allowed but must be kept on a leash at all times. Visit during spring or fall for the best temperatures and seasonal foliage.

attractions.name: Memory Grove Park
attractions.description: Established in 1924 as a war memorial at the mouth of City Creek Canyon, Memory Grove Park serves as a peaceful retreat and a tribute to Utah's fallen military service members. The park is renowned for its lush vegetation, monuments, and a scenic stream that follows the City Creek Canyon trail. It features a variety of neoclassical memorials, including a marble pagoda and a meditation chapel, set within a serene landscape popular for walking and reflection. (Wikipedia, SLC.gov)

attractions.days_open: Daily
attractions.admission: Free (Salt Lake City Arts Council, SLC.gov)
highlights: The Pagoda: A classical marble structure supported by eight columns, made from the same Vermont quarry as the Lincoln Memorial.
Meditation Chapel: A Greek-temple style chapel built from Georgian marble with sculpted bronze doors.
Harbor of Beauty: A memorial pond dedicated to the memory of fallen American sailors.
145th Field Artillery Monument: A tall gray granite shaft with a bas-relief plaque featuring horse-drawn artillery.
Off-leash Dog Area: A popular section of the park where dogs can run freely.
City Creek Canyon Trail: A scenic path for jogging and walking that leads further into the canyon.
attractions.tips: The park is very dog-friendly, particularly the off-leash area. Parking is available near the front gate and along Canyon Road; however, the park itself is gated, and authorized vehicles may need to use a call box to enter. It is a popular spot for wedding photography and offers beautiful views, especially during the golden hour. (Memorial House, Brandon Burk Photography)

attractions.description: The Park City Museum is a nonprofit organization dedicated to preserving, protecting, and promoting Park City's rich history and heritage. Located in the historic City Hall, it offers interactive exhibits that explore the town's transformation from a silver mining hub to a world-class ski destination. (Park City Museum, Utah Division of Arts & Museums)

attractions.hours: Monday: 10 AM - 5 PM, Tuesday: 10 AM - 5 PM, Wednesday: 10 AM - 5 PM, Thursday: 10 AM - 5 PM, Friday: 10 AM - 5 PM, Saturday: 10 AM - 5 PM, Sunday: 10 AM - 5 PM (Park City Museum)

attractions.name: America First Field (formerly Rio Tinto Stadium)
attractions.description: America First Field is a premier soccer-specific stadium and the proud home of Real Salt Lake (MLS) and the Utah Royals (NWSL). Opened in 2008, the venue features a natural grass surface and a capacity of over 20,000, serving as a hub for professional sports and major concerts in the Salt Lake Valley (America First Field, Wikipedia).

attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Saturday (Main Team Store and Box Office); Daily for scheduled events (Real Salt Lake Guide).
attractions.admission: Individual tickets typically range from $16–$36+ depending on the event and seat location (SeatGeek, SeatGeek). Season ticket members receive up to 30-33% off individual pricing (Real Salt Lake).
highlights: Home pitch for Real Salt Lake (MLS) and Utah Royals (NWSL) (America First Field).
State-of-the-art natural grass playing surface (Wikipedia).
Hosted major events like the 2009 MLS All-Star Game and CONCACAF Champions League Finals (Wikipedia).
Beddy’s Plaza fan zone featuring live music, food, and pre-match activities (Real Salt Lake Guide).
Eco-friendly initiatives including a large-scale solar array (Wikipedia).
Premium seating options including luxury suites and the 100 Lions Club (Real Salt Lake Guide).
attractions.tips: Follow the strict 'No Bag' policy (bags must be smaller than 4”x6”x2”). Arrive early to enjoy pre-match festivities at Beddy’s Plaza. Use the free ADA-compliant shuttle from Sandy City Hall for easier parking (America First Field Info, Real Salt Lake Guide).
attractions.nearby: Hale Center Theatre, Loveland Living Planet Aquarium, The Shops at South Town, and various dining options like Crown Burgers and Slackwater (Tripadvisor, Visit Utah).

attractions.name: American Fork Canyon (Uinta-Wasatch-Cache National Forest)
attractions.description: American Fork Canyon is a 46,000-acre recreational haven in the Wasatch Mountains, climbing from 4,800 feet to over 10,000 feet. It features the famous Alpine Loop Scenic Byway, the underground wonders of Timpanogos Cave, and multiple reservoirs for fishing and camping. (Forest Service, Utah Valley)

attractions.days_open: Daily (Lower canyon open year-round; Alpine Loop and upper canyon typically open June–October) (Utah Valley)
attractions.admission: $6 per vehicle (3-day pass), $12 (7-day pass), $45 (annual pass); America the Beautiful passes are accepted (Forest Service, Casago)
attractions.phone: (801) 785-3563 (Pleasant Grove Ranger District) or (801) 756-5238 (Timpanogos Cave) (Forest Service)
highlights: Alpine Loop Scenic Byway
Timpanogos Cave National Monument
Tibble Fork and Silver Lake Flat Reservoirs
Mount Timpanogos and Lone Peak Wilderness hiking
Dramatic fall foliage displays
Cascade Springs nature area
attractions.tips: Book Timpanogos Cave tours weeks in advance online at recreation.gov; the cave remains 45°F year-round, so bring a jacket; check road conditions for seasonal Alpine Loop closures. (Utah Valley)
attractions.nearby: Cascade Springs, Wasatch Mountain State Park, Provo Canyon, Bridal Veil Falls, Utah Lake (Forest Service)

attractions.name: Antelope Island State Park Official Website
attractions.description: Antelope Island State Park is the largest of ten islands within the Great Salt Lake, offering spectacular views of the lake and surrounding mountains. It is a vital habitat for diverse wildlife, most notably the Antelope Island bison herd, as well as pronghorn, bighorn sheep, and millions of waterfowl. Wikipedia, Official Website

attractions.hours: Park: 6:00 am – 10:00 pm daily; Visitor Information Center: 9:00 am – 5:00 pm daily; Fielding Garr Ranch: 10:00 am – 5:00 pm daily Official Website
attractions.days_open: Daily (closed Thanksgiving and Christmas) Official Website
attractions.admission: $15 per vehicle (up to 8 people), $10 for seniors (65+), $5 per motorcycle, $3 per person for bicyclists and pedestrians Official Website
highlights: Antelope Island Bison Herd (one of the largest publicly owned herds in the U.S.) Wikipedia
Historic Fielding Garr Ranch Official Website
Frary Peak hiking trail (highest point on the island) Wikipedia
Bridger Bay Beach for swimming and Great Salt Lake views Official Website
Abundant birdwatching opportunities including burrowing owls and waterfowl Wikipedia
Extensive multi-use trail system for hiking and mountain biking Official Website
attractions.tips: Beware of biting gnats (no-see-ums) in late spring; check the park's current conditions page. Rideshare services like Uber and Lyft do not provide pickups from the park, so ensure you have a rental or personal vehicle. Bring plenty of water as it is a desert environment with limited resources. Official Website
attractions.nearby: Bear River Migratory Bird Refuge, Hill Aerospace Museum, Lagoon Amusement Park, Salt Lake City Wikipedia, TripAdvisor

attractions.name: Bar X (Official Website)
attractions.description: Bar X is an intimate Prohibition-style cocktail bar located in the heart of downtown Salt Lake City. Originally opened in 1933 following the end of Prohibition, it was revitalized in 2011 to focus on a high-end craft cocktail program and an extensive list of fine spirits. The bar maintains a classic speakeasy atmosphere with dim lighting and a curated menu of both traditional and modern libations. (Official Website, Imbibe Magazine)

attractions.days_open: Daily (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)

Extensive menu of classic and craft cocktails prepared by skilled bartenders. (Official Website)
Intimate speakeasy-style atmosphere with historic decor. (Official Website)
Connected to sister establishment Beer Bar, which offers a wide selection of craft beers and food. (Gastronomic Salt Lake City)
Offers "Back Bar" reservations for groups seeking a more private experience. (Official Website)
A wide selection of fine spirits available for sipping or mixing. (Official Website)
attractions.tips: The bar is 21+ only. It is a popular spot and can get crowded quickly on weekend nights, so arrive early to secure a seat. For a more casual vibe or food options like sausages and fries, you can head through the adjoining door to Beer Bar. (Tripadvisor, Imbibe Magazine)
attractions.nearby: Beer Bar, Eccles Theater, Salt Lake City Public Library, City Creek Center, The Copper Onion (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Beehive Distilling
attractions.description: Established in 2013, Beehive Distilling is Utah's first gin distillery in over a century, known for its creative approach to spirits. The distillery produces a range of premium gins, whiskeys, and organic vodka, emphasizing local ingredients and artisanal craftsmanship in a revamped industrial space. Official Website, Visit Salt Lake

attractions.name: Bell Canyon (Bells Canyon)
attractions.description: Bell Canyon is a scenic drainage in the Wasatch Range located on the southeast edge of the Salt Lake Valley in Sandy, Utah. It is renowned for its stunning granite peaks, a peaceful reservoir, and dramatic waterfalls, offering a variety of trails ranging from easy strolls to strenuous climbs into the Lone Peak Wilderness. Road Trip Ryan, Visit Salt Lake

attractions.region: Little Cottonwood Canyon Area / Salt Lake Valley Visit Salt Lake
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Tuesday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Wednesday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Thursday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Friday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Saturday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Sunday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM Sandy City

Upper Bell Canyon Falls

Access to the Lone Peak Wilderness and Lone Peak Cirque

attractions.tips: No dogs or horses are allowed due to watershed protection; violators may be fined. Arrive early, especially on weekends, as the trailheads are extremely popular and parking fills up quickly. Wear micro-spikes or crampons if hiking in winter, as trails can become very icy. Sandy City, Utah Hiking Beauty

attractions.name: Big Cottonwood Canyon
attractions.description: Big Cottonwood Canyon is a stunning 15-mile-long glacial canyon in the Wasatch Range known for its dramatic rock formations and diverse outdoor recreation. As a critical watershed providing over 60% of Salt Lake City's drinking water, it offers world-class skiing, hiking to alpine lakes, and premier rock climbing while strictly prohibiting domestic animals (Wikipedia, U.S. Forest Service).
attractions.address: Big Cottonwood Canyon Rd, Salt Lake City, UT 84121
attractions.region: Salt Lake Ranger District, Wasatch Mountains
attractions.hours: Canyon Road: 24/7 (subject to seasonal closures); Silver Lake Visitor Center: 11:00 AM – 4:30 PM daily (May–August); Salt Lake Ranger District: Mon–Fri 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM (U.S. Forest Service, Cottonwood Canyons Foundation).
attractions.days_open: Daily
attractions.admission: Free entry to the canyon; parking at designated trailheads requires a Recreation Enhancement Act (REA) fee: $10 (3-day pass), $20 (7-day pass), or a valid America the Beautiful/National Parks Pass ($80/year) (Salt Lake Climbers Alliance, Cottonwood Canyons Foundation).
highlights: Brighton and Solitude Mountain Resorts for world-class skiing and snowboarding (Wikipedia).
Silver Lake Visitor Center and its accessible boardwalk for wildlife viewing (Cottonwood Canyons Foundation).
Scenic hikes to alpine destinations like Donut Falls, Lake Blanche, and Twin Lakes (Utah.com).
Premier rock climbing locations including Storm Mountain and the S-Curve area (Wikipedia).
Summer access to Park City via the breathtaking Guardsman Pass Scenic Backway (U.S. Forest Service).
Fishing and picnicking at numerous developed sites along Big Cottonwood Creek (Alta Chalets).
attractions.tips: Dogs and other pets are strictly prohibited even in vehicles to protect the watershed; swimming and wading in lakes or streams are also forbidden. Carpooling is highly recommended as parking lots often fill by 8:30 AM, and some areas require parking reservations during peak winter periods (U.S. Forest Service, UDOT).
attractions.nearby: Little Cottonwood Canyon, Solitude Mountain Resort, Brighton Resort, Guardsman Pass, Park City (Wikipedia, Tripadvisor).

The brewery features a rustic mountain lodge atmosphere complete with a cozy fireplace, an eclectic collection of vintage scooters, and an outdoor patio offering majestic views of the nearby mountains. (Bohemian Brewery)

Created by Thomas Battersby Child, Jr. between 1945 and 1963, this hidden urban oasis was designed as a retreat of faith and artistic expression, tucked away in the middle of a city block behind residential houses and businesses (Official Website, KSL).

attractions.region: East Central / Central City (Official Website, Daily Utah Chronicle)
attractions.hours: April–September: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM daily; October–March: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM daily (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Daily (except New Year's Day, Thanksgiving, and Christmas) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website, Wikipedia)
highlights: The Sphinx: A famous sculpture featuring the face of Joseph Smith on a lion's body (Wikipedia)
Engraved Stones: Over 70 stones meticulously carved with scriptures, poems, and philosophical texts (Official Website)

Visionary Art Environment: The only site in Utah designated with this artistic classification (SLC.gov)
Thomas Child's Workshop: The site of the artist's original workshop where he used innovative tools like oxyacetylene torches to carve stone (Official Website)

attractions.tips: Look for the small sign and iron gate located between two residential houses on 500 South, as the garden is easily missed. Free street parking is available along 500 South, and the garden features paved, accessible walking paths. Visitor brochures and audio tours are often available to help explain the rich symbolism of the sculptures (Utah Discover, Utah's Adventure Family).

attractions.name: Silver Lake Flat Reservoir
attractions.description: Silver Lake Flat Reservoir is a high-mountain reservoir nestled in American Fork Canyon within the Uinta-Wasatch-Cache National Forest. It offers a stunning alpine setting with scenic views of Mount Timpanogos and White Baldy, making it a popular destination for non-motorized water activities such as kayaking and fishing. The area also serves as a key trailhead for hikers venturing into the Lone Peak Wilderness. (Forest Service, Utah Hiking Beauty)

attractions.region: American Fork Canyon / Uinta-Wasatch-Cache National Forest
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily during the summer season; Ranger District Office hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM (Forest Service)
attractions.days_open: Daily during the summer season (typically May through October) (Utah Hiking Beauty)
attractions.admission: $10 per vehicle (3-day pass), $20 per vehicle (7-day pass), or $60 (season pass); America the Beautiful National Parks Passes are also accepted (Forest Service)
highlights: Breathtaking mountain reflections of Mount Timpanogos and White Baldy. (YouTube)
Excellent fishing for Arctic Grayling, Rainbow, Brook, and Cutthroat Trout. (Junesucker.com)
Popular for non-motorized watercraft, including kayaking and paddleboarding. (Recreation.gov)
Access to the Silver Lake Trail #036 leading into the Lone Peak Wilderness. (Forest Service)
Dispersed camping opportunities in designated areas near the reservoir. (Forest Service)
Secluded alpine environment surrounded by dense aspen and pine forests. (Brandon Burk Photography)
attractions.tips: The final 3 miles of access on Forest Road 008 is a rugged dirt road where high-clearance vehicles are recommended. Arrive early on summer weekends as the limited parking areas fill quickly. Check road conditions before visiting, as heavy snow can keep the gate closed until mid-July. Always bring bug spray for mosquitoes and plenty of water for the high elevation. (Forest Service, Brandon Burk Photography)

attractions.name: The Great Saltair (The Great Saltair)
attractions.description: The Great Saltair is a historic music and entertainment venue located on the southern shore of the Great Salt Lake. Originally established in 1893 as a grand resort and amusement park, the current facility (the third iteration) functions as a premier concert hall and outdoor amphitheater hosting major musical acts and festivals. (The Great Saltair, Wikipedia))

attractions.days_open: Open for scheduled events; site grounds and nearby state park accessible daily (The Great Saltair, Utah State Parks)
attractions.admission: Varies by event (ticketed concerts and festivals); Parking is typically $10-$20 per vehicle (The Great Saltair)

Large indoor concert hall and expansive outdoor amphitheater (The Great Saltair)
Direct access to the shores of the Great Salt Lake for sightseeing (The Great Saltair)

Host venue for the annual Das Energi Festival and other major electronic dance music events (The Great Saltair)
attractions.tips: Arrive early for concerts as parking can fill quickly; the venue has a strict no re-entry policy and is standing room only. Be prepared for the unique 'lake smell' and seasonal brine flies near the shoreline. (The Great Saltair, Tripadvisor)

attractions.description: The Leonardo was a non-profit, community-powered museum of creativity and innovation that merged science, technology, and art into interactive exhibits. It encouraged hands-on learning, problem-solving, and social exchange to spark curiosity in visitors of all ages. The museum was housed in the historic former Salt Lake City Public Library building until its permanent closure due to aging infrastructure. Tripadvisor, The Leonardo Official Site

attractions.name: The Rest (at Bodega)
attractions.description: The Rest is a subterranean speakeasy and restaurant located beneath Bodega in downtown Salt Lake City, offering an intimate and moody atmosphere reminiscent of a library or a hunting lodge. Guests enter through a hidden "restroom" door in the upstairs tavern to find a space filled with dark wood, taxidermy, and spinning vinyl records. The menu features elevated New American cuisine alongside a sophisticated "cocktail alchemy" program and an extensive whiskey selection.

attractions.name: Timpanogos Cave National Monument (NPS)
attractions.description: Timpanogos Cave National Monument protects a scientifically important high-elevation, fault-controlled cave system in the rugged American Fork Canyon. The monument features three highly-decorated limestone caves—Hansen Cave, Middle Cave, and Timpanogos Cave—connected by man-made tunnels and accessed via a strenuous 1.5-mile paved trail (NPS, NPS).
attractions.address: 2038 W. Alpine Loop Road (NPS)
attractions.hours: Visitor Center: 7:00 AM – 5:30 PM; Cave Tours: 7:00 AM – 3:30 PM; Picnic Areas: Sunrise – 10:00 PM (NPS)
attractions.days_open: Daily during the season (late May through mid-October) (NPS)
attractions.admission: Park Entrance: Free; Cave Tour: $12.00 (Adult 12+), $7.00 (Junior 2-11), $2.00 (Infant 0-1), $6.00 (Senior/Access Pass holders); Lantern Tour: $17.00; Introduction to Caving: $22.00 (NPS, Wikivoyage)
highlights: The "Great Heart of Timpanogos" stalactite formation (Wikipedia)
Abundant helictites and aragonite crystals throughout the cave system (NPS)
Strenuous 1.5-mile paved access trail with a 1,100-foot elevation gain (NPS)
Three interconnected natural caves: Hansen, Middle, and Timpanogos (NPS)
Panoramic views of American Fork Canyon and Utah Valley (Expedia)
attractions.tips: Wear a light jacket as the cave is 45°F year-round; book tickets up to 30 days in advance via Recreation.gov as they frequently sell out; no strollers or wheeled vehicles are allowed on the steep trail; arrive at the visitor center at least 1.5 hours before your tour time to hike up (NPS, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: American Fork Canyon (Tripadvisor), Tibble Fork Reservoir (Tripadvisor), Alpine Loop Scenic Byway (Utah Valley)

Converted into a festival marketplace in 1972, it features unique Spanish-Mission architecture, winding hallways, and a prominent 97-foot water tower that serves as a city icon. The center is a noteworthy example of adaptive reuse, blending local boutiques and national retailers with rich historical preservation Trolley Square, Wikipedia.

attractions.description: The University of Utah is a public research university and the flagship institution of higher education in the state, founded in 1850.
